title: "WHERE a=? AND b IN (?,?,...) AND c>?" query using the seekscan optimization sometimes returns extra rows.
poc: CREATE TABLE t1(a TEXT, b INT, c INT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY(a,b,c));

WITH RECURSIVE c(x) AS (VALUES(0) UNION ALL SELECT x+1 FROM c WHERE x<1997)
    INSERT INTO t1(a,b,c) SELECT printf('xyz%d',x/10),x/6,x FROM c;
SELECT a,b,c FROM t1 WHERE b IN (235, 345) AND c<=3 AND a='abc' ORDER BY a, b;
key_bug_features: Seekscan optimization incorrectly returns extra rows in query results.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(a TEXT, b INTEGER, c INTEGER NOT NULL, d REAL DEFAULT 0.0, PRIMARY KEY(a, b));
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c) VALUES('test1', 100, 1);
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c) VALUES('test2', 200, 2);
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c) VALUES('test3', 300, 3);
SELECT a, b, c FROM t2 WHERE b IN (100, 200) AND c < 3 ORDER BY c DESC;
